name: Fagioli e Gabmeri (Canellini Beans with Sauteed Shrimp)
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Dried white beans (such as cannellini, Great Northern or navy beans)
2 large Garlic cloves, peeled and chopped
6 large Fresh sage leaves, or 1 tsp Dried whole leaf sage
Salt and black pepper to taste
1 lb Medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
recipeInstructions:
Note: The beans in this recipe will need to be soaked overnight.
1. Place the beans in a non-aluminum container and cover with at least 5 inches of cold water. Soak overnight and then drain well.
2. In a large pot that can comfortably hold all ingredients, combine the drained beans (excluding salt), olive oil, garlic, pancetta, sage, and tomato. Add enough cold water to cover everything by about 3 inches.
3. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over low heat. Cook for about 1 hour or longer until the beans are mostly absorbed into the liquid and remain moist and succulent. Season with salt to taste.
4. Serve the beans hot, drizzled with olive oil, or at room temperature.
5. For the shrimp: Heat some olive oil in a separate pan over medium heat. Add a pinch of salt and pepper, then sauté the shrimp for about 2 minutes, stirring frequently until just cooked through but still tender.
This dish serves 6 to 8 people.
